title: Agriphyllum echinatum
intro: Agriphyllum echinatum, also known as Echinated Wheat Grass, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family, Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, lowering blood pressure, and acting as an antioxidant. Bioactive constituents of Agriphyllum echinatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, as well as dietary supplements, to promote overall well-being and health.
taxonomy: Agriphyllum echinatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Cynodonteae, which is a group of grasses. The genus Agriphyllum is part of the subfamily Chloridoideae. This classification places Agriphyllum echinatum within the larger family of grasses, Poaceae. Further classification includes it being a member of the order Poales.
